perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as confidential all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security' it removes a layer of protection that was being afforded. This omission now introduces a contradiction with clause 14, which acknowledges the confidential nature of the information. Without the specific obligation to take 'reasonable steps', it becomes unclear what actions the recipient must take to uphold the confidentiality, potentially undermining the acknowledgement in clause 14.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient recognises and acknowledges the Disclosing Party’s representation of the competitive value and confidential nature of the Information and Materials and that any use or 4 disclosure of the Information or Materials o ther than as expressly authorised by this agreement will be detrimental to the Disclosing Party, and may result in the Disclosing Party suffering substantial and irreparable loss and damage. Without affecting any other rights or remedies that any party may have, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that due to the character of the Information to be made available hereunder, damages would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers of the provisions of this agreement, and that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this agreement by the Recipient or any of i ts directors, officers, employees, agents or advisers and that no proof of special damage or special damages shall be necessary for the enforcement of this agreement. Nothing contained in this paragraph shall, however, be construed as a waiver by the Disc losing Party of any other rights, including without limitation, rights for damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the recipient to 'take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security' of the information. The changed text removes this requirement, only stating that the recipient will 'treat and safeguard as confidential' the information. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which acknowledges the 'confidential nature of the Information' and that unauthorized use will be detrimental. Without the explicit obligation to take 'reasonable steps' as stated in the original text, the changed text introduces uncertainty about the recipient's required actions, thus weakening the protection and contradicting the acknowledgement of the information's confidential nature. This is in line with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If the Disclosing Party so requests in writing at any time, the Recipient will forthwith return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Information and Materials toge ther with all copies, records, analyses, memoranda or other notes to the extent containing or reflecting any of the Information made by it or its directors, officers, employees, agents of advisers. In addition the Recipient, its directors, officers, emplo yees, agents and advisers will erase all Information from any computer, word processor or other device containing Information in its or their possession, cu stody or control.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Disclosing Party so requests in writing at any time, the Recipient will forthwith return to the Disclosing Party all Information and Materials toge ther with all copies, records, analyses, memoranda or other notes to the extent containing or reflecting any of the Information made by it or its directors, officers, employees, agents of advisers.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the obligation to 'destroy' the information and also removing the addition to 'erase all Information from any computer, word processor or other device containing Information in its or their possession, custody or control', there is a contradiction with Clause 7. Clause 7 states 'All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.' By not destroying or erasing, there is a question now, of how the Recipient can adhere to Clause 7, as the only thing they can do is return it physically, but not erase any local copies. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Recipient's obligation to 'destroy' information and erase it from devices. Clause 7 (the contradicted text) states the Information remains the Disclosing Party's property and the Recipient has no rights to it. By removing the destruction/erasure obligation, the changed text contradicts Clause 7 because the Recipient can retain copies, potentially conflicting with the Disclosing Party's ownership and the Recipient's lack of rights as now they are in possession of information that should only be owned by the disclosing party.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy, completeness or reasonableness of the Information or Materials, and the Recipient agree s that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its respective directors, officers, employees, advisers and agents shall have any liability to it, its directors, officers, employees, advisers or agents resulting from the furnishing or use of the Information or Materials. In furnishing or making available the Information or Materials, the Disclosing Party undertakes no obligation to provide the Recipient, its directors, officers, employees, advisers or agents with access to any additional information or to update the Information or Materials or correct inaccuracies therein which may become apparent and neither the Disclosing Party nor any other person shall be liable for any direct, indirect or consequential loss or damage suffered by any person as a result o f relying on any statement contained in or omitted from the Information or Materials, whether caused by the negligence of the Disclosing Party or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the Information or Materials, and the Recipient agree s that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its respective directors, officers, employees, advisers and agents shall have any liability to it, its directors, officers, employees, advisers or agents resulting from the furnishing or use of the Information or Materials. In furnishing or making available the Information or Materials, the Disclosing Party undertakes no obligation to provide the Recipient, its directors, officers, employees, advisers or agents with access to any additional information or to update the Information or Materials or correct inaccuracies therein which may become apparent and neither the Disclosing Party nor any other person shall be liable for any direct, indirect or consequential loss or damage suffered by any person as a result o f relying on any statement contained in or omitted from the Information or Materials, whether caused by the negligence of the Disclosing Party or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting completeness or reasonableness of the information, it creates a contradiction with Clause 11. If the Disclosing party furnishes the information that the receiving party depends on to be in compliance. And then, it is not followed through, this omisison creates a loop hole that the receiving party can use when they are not able to perform their duties as per the rest of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees to indemnify, and to keep indemnified, each of the Disclosing Party, its direc tors, officers, employees, advisers and agents and each of them against all actions, claims, liabilities, damages, costs, charges and expenses which the Disclosing Party, its directors, officers, employees, advisers and agents (or any of them) may suffer o r incur in connection with or arising out of any breach by the Recipient or by any other person to whom the Recipient has disclosed or given access to any part of the Information, of any of the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Disclosing Party makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy, completeness, or reasonableness of the Information. The changed text removes "completeness or reasonableness". This omission, as per the explanation, creates a loophole. If the recipient depends on the information for compliance, the omission of completeness and reasonableness now contradicts the section requiring the recipient to indemnify the disclosing party against breaches arising from disclosed information, because the recipient can now argue the information wasn't complete or reasonable, which they were relying on. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction as it weakens the disclosing party's protection and could lead to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: The Buyers agree that they will not (collectively or individually and/ or either directly or indirectly) solicit the employment or engagement or attempt to solicit the employment or engagement of the Key Personnel or otherwise employ or engage any of the Vendors Key Personnel (other than in the Target or otherwise following the successful acquisition of the Target) in any capacity whatsoever for 12 months following the date of this letter or the date on w hich the proposed acquisition is aborted (whichever is the latter).
perturbation.changed_text: The Buyers agree that they will not (collectively or individually and/ or either directly or indirectly) solicit the employment or engagement or attempt to solicit the employment or engagement of the Key Personnel or otherwise employ or engage any of the Vendors Key Personnel (other than in the Target or otherwise following the successful acquisition of the Target) in any capacity whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'for 12 months following the date of this letter or the date on w hich the proposed acquisition is aborted (whichever is the latter)' removes the time limit on the restriction. The Buyers now 'agree that they will not...solicit the employment' indefinitely. This creates a contradiction with Clause 15, which states 'The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that its obligations under this agreement shall be continuing and, in particular, they shall survive the termination of this Agreement and shall not be affected by any discussions between the Recipient and the Disclosing Party regarding the Information or Materials.' The act of removing this time constraint on this clause. This clause contradicts the original clause now, as Clause 15 mentions that all obligations are not affected by the termination of this agreement, but the change in this perturbation implies that they are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that its obligations under this agreement shall be continuing and, in particular, they shall survive the termination of this Agreement and shall not be affected by any discussions between the Recipient and the Disclosing Party regarding the Information or Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a 12-month time limit on soliciting Key Personnel. The changed text removes this time limit, making the restriction indefinite. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that obligations survive the termination of the agreement. With the time limit removed, the indefinite restriction on soliciting Key Personnel imposed by the changed text now clashes with the idea that obligations generally continue after termination, creating an inconsistency regarding whether or not this obligation is continuing.
